overview: Re-joins multiple XLIFF documents that were previously split (e.g., by the **XLIFF Splitter Step**) back into one or more consolidated documents. Files are grouped by a naming pattern based on a configurable marker string; the first document in each group provides the skeleton (content before the first `<file>` and after the last `<file>`), while subsequent documents contribute their `<file>` elements. Takes raw documents and sends back raw documents.

inputFileMarker.help:
The marker string used to identify and group files that belong together. Files whose names contain this marker followed by a numeric index (e.g., `File1_PART001.xlf`, `File1_PART002.xlf`) are treated as parts of the same document and joined together.

Files whose names do **not** contain this marker are skipped entirely.

outputFileMarker.help: The marker string that replaces the input file marker (and its numeric index) in the output filename. For example, if the input marker is `_PART` and the output marker is `_CONCAT`, then `File1_PART001.xlf` and `File1_PART002.xlf` produce `File1_CONCAT.xlf`.
notes: If files with the generated output names already exist in the output directory, they will be **overwritten without warning**.

processingNotes: Files are joined in the order they arrive in the pipeline, **regardless of the index number** added by the XLIFF Splitter Step. Pipeline processing is sequential.
The first document in a naming-pattern group provides the XML skeleton — content before the first `<file>` element and after the last `<file>` element.

Subsequent documents in the same group contribute only their `<file>` elements (and any content between multiple `<file>` elements). Content before their first `<file>` and after their last `<file>` is ignored, since the XLIFF Splitter Step duplicates this content into every part.
Files whose names do not contain the input file marker are skipped.

examples.title: Joining split XLIFF files
examples.description: Two groups of split files are joined back together. `File3.xlf` is skipped because it lacks the `_PART` marker.
examples.output:
```
C:\Project\FolderABC\File1_CONCAT.xlf
C:\Project\FolderABC\File2_CONCAT.xlf

File3.xlf → Skipped (filename does not contain "_PART")
```
